recipes.title: Borscht
recipes.description: Iconic Eastern Slavic beetroot soup with beef, cabbage and root vegetables, served with sour cream and dill.
steps: Simmer the beef in salted water with bay leaves for 90 minutes, skimming any foam.
Sauté the onion and carrot in oil until soft, then add the beetroot, tomato paste and vinegar and cook for 10 minutes.
Lift the beef from the broth, dice it, and return it to the pot.
Add the potatoes to the broth and simmer for 10 minutes.
Add the cabbage and the beetroot mixture and cook for another 15 minutes.
Stir in the crushed garlic, season well, and let the soup rest off heat for 10 minutes.
Serve hot with a generous spoonful of sour cream and chopped dill on each bowl.

recipes.title: Beef Stroganoff
recipes.description: Tender strips of beef in a creamy mushroom and sour cream sauce, traditionally served over noodles or with potatoes.
steps: Slice the beef into thin strips against the grain and toss with flour, salt and pepper.
Heat the oil in a large skillet and sear the beef in batches over high heat for 1 to 2 minutes per batch.
Remove the beef, lower the heat, and add the butter, onions and mushrooms; cook until golden.
Pour in the beef stock and Dijon mustard, scraping the pan, and simmer until slightly reduced.
Stir in the sour cream and warm through without boiling.
Return the beef and any juices to the pan and warm for 2 minutes.
Adjust seasoning, sprinkle with parsley, and serve over buttered noodles or with mashed potatoes.

recipes.title: Pelmeni
recipes.description: Russian dumplings of thin unleavened dough wrapped around a seasoned meat filling, served with sour cream or butter.
steps: Mix the flour, eggs, water and salt to a firm dough, knead for 10 minutes, wrap and rest for 30 minutes.
Combine both meats with the onion, garlic, ice water, salt and pepper until sticky.
Roll the dough very thin and cut into 6 cm rounds with a cutter.
Place a teaspoon of filling on each round, fold in half and pinch the edges, then bring the two corners together.
Boil the pelmeni in salted water for 5 to 7 minutes until they float and the filling is cooked.
Drain and toss with butter, then serve with sour cream and freshly cracked pepper.

recipes.title: Solyanka
recipes.description: Tangy Russian soup with smoked meats or fish, pickles, capers, olives and lemon, served with sour cream.
steps: Sauté the onion in butter until soft, add the tomato paste and cook for 3 minutes until darkened.
Add the diced pickles and brine and simmer for 5 minutes.
Pour in the beef stock and bring to a simmer.
Add all of the smoked sausage, ham and cooked beef and simmer for 15 minutes.
Stir in the capers and olives and let the soup rest off heat for 10 minutes.
Ladle into bowls and finish each with a slice of lemon, a spoon of sour cream and chopped dill.

recipes.title: Olivier Salad
recipes.description: Russian potato salad with chicken, peas, pickles and eggs in a creamy mayonnaise dressing, a New Year staple.
steps: Make sure the potatoes, carrots, eggs and chicken are fully cooked and cooled before dicing into 1 cm cubes.
Combine all of the diced ingredients with the peas and onion in a large bowl.
Whisk the mayonnaise with the Dijon mustard, salt and pepper.
Fold the dressing through the salad until everything is evenly coated.
Refrigerate for at least 1 hour to let the flavours meld.
Stir through the dill and adjust seasoning before serving.

recipes.title: Blini
recipes.description: Thin yeasted Russian pancakes traditionally served with sour cream, smoked salmon, caviar or jam.
steps: Stir the yeast and sugar into the warm milk and let bloom for 10 minutes.
Whisk in both flours, the salt and the egg yolks, then the melted butter, to a smooth batter.
Cover and leave to rise in a warm place for 1 hour until bubbly.
Whip the egg whites to soft peaks and fold into the risen batter.
Heat a small skillet, brush with butter, and pour in a thin layer of batter.
Cook for about 1 minute per side until golden, transferring to a warm plate.
Serve warm topped with sour cream and your choice of smoked salmon, caviar or jam.

recipes.title: Pirozhki
recipes.description: Hand-sized Russian baked or fried buns stuffed with savory or sweet fillings, ideal as a snack or lunch.
steps: Bloom the yeast and sugar in warm milk, then mix with the flour, salt, egg and melted butter.
Knead to a soft dough and let rise covered for 1 hour until doubled.
Brown the ground beef with the chopped onion, season with salt and pepper, and stir in the chopped boiled eggs.
Knock back the dough and divide into 16 pieces. Flatten each into a disc.
Place a heaped tablespoon of filling on each disc, fold in the edges and pinch to seal, then place seam-side down.
Let the pirozhki rise for 20 minutes, brush with egg wash, and bake at 200°C for 20 to 25 minutes until golden.

recipes.title: Shchi
recipes.description: Traditional Russian cabbage soup with beef, root vegetables and dill, often eaten with rye bread and sour cream.
steps: Simmer the beef in salted water with bay leaves for 90 minutes, skimming any foam.
Lift out the beef, dice it and reserve, keeping the broth.
Sauté the onion and carrot in oil, then add the diced tomato and cook for 5 minutes.
Add the cabbage to the broth and simmer for 15 minutes.
Add the potatoes and the sautéed vegetables and simmer for 15 more minutes.
Return the diced beef, stir in the crushed garlic, season well, and rest off heat for 10 minutes.
Serve hot topped with sour cream and chopped dill.

recipes.title: Kasha
recipes.description: Russian buckwheat porridge cooked in stock or milk, served as a savory side or sweet breakfast.
steps: Rinse the buckwheat groats under cold water and drain.
Toast the buckwheat in a dry pot for 2 minutes until aromatic.
Add the water and salt, bring to a boil, then cover and simmer over low heat for 15 minutes until the water is absorbed.
Meanwhile, sauté the onion, carrot and mushrooms in butter until golden.
Stir the vegetables through the cooked buckwheat with the remaining butter.
Season with black pepper, sprinkle with parsley, and serve as a side or main.

recipes.title: Ukha
recipes.description: Clear Russian fish soup made with freshwater fish, root vegetables, and herbs, kept light and unthickened.
steps: Simmer the fish heads and bones with the whole onion, bay leaves, peppercorns and salt in the water for 40 minutes.
Strain the broth carefully back into a clean pot, discarding the solids.
Add the potatoes and sliced carrot and simmer for 10 minutes.
Add the fish fillets cut into large chunks and simmer for 8 to 10 minutes until just cooked.
Pour in the vodka and stir gently to retain the broth's clarity.
Stir in most of the dill and parsley, taste for salt, and let rest off heat for 5 minutes.
Serve in bowls with a slice of lemon and the remaining herbs.

recipes.title: Kvas
recipes.description: Lightly fermented Russian beverage made from rye bread, water, sugar and yeast, mildly tangy and refreshing.
steps: Toast the rye bread in a 180°C oven for 15 minutes until very dark, almost burnt.
Place the toasted bread in a large jar and pour over the boiling water.
Cover loosely with a clean cloth and leave to infuse for 8 hours at room temperature.
Strain the liquid through a fine sieve back into the jar, pressing the bread to extract flavor.
Stir in the sugar and the yeast, then add the raisins and a few mint leaves.
Cover with cloth and ferment at room temperature for 12 to 16 hours until lightly bubbly.
Strain into bottles, refrigerate, and serve very cold.

recipes.title: Kotleta po-pozharsky
recipes.description: Classical Russian fried chicken cutlet of minced chicken bound with cream, breadcrumbs and butter, golden and juicy.
steps: Soak the white bread in the cream until softened, then squeeze gently and add to the minced chicken.
Mix in the grated onion, salt, pepper and cold butter cubes, working briefly so the butter stays in chunks.
Chill the mixture for 30 minutes for the butter to firm.
Shape into oval cutlets about 2 cm thick.
Dip each cutlet in beaten egg and coat thickly in breadcrumbs.
Heat butter and oil in a large skillet and fry the cutlets over medium heat for 4 to 5 minutes per side until deep golden and cooked through.
Rest for 3 minutes and serve with mashed potatoes or kasha.
